Text:
Hi everyone,

I’ve been using a Samsung Galaxy A71 for the last 3 years (running One UI 5), and honestly, it’s still a solid phone. But I’m thinking of upgrading soon and have narrowed it down to two options:

1. Galaxy A56


2. Oppo Reno 13 (or maybe wait for the Reno 14, which isn’t out yet in Pakistan)

Before I decide, I have a few questions for people who’ve used ColorOS 15:

1. Does ColorOS have anything like Samsung’s Secure Folder, where you can keep private apps and photos separate?

2. Does it offer Modes and Routines, or something similar to automate settings based on time, location, etc.?

3. Is there any feature to lower the minimum volume? I usually fall asleep listening to podcasts, and I find Samsung’s lowest volume still too loud sometimes.

4. How good is the ColorOS Gallery app compared to Samsung’s?

Can you hide photos/videos easily?

Can you create custom albums?

Can you rename photos/videos directly in the app?

5. Does the file manager let you sync or access files directly from Google Drive?

6. Can you clone apps (e.g., use two WhatsApp/Facebook accounts on the same phone) easily?

7. Lastly, how does the camera quality compare between the Galaxy A56 and Oppo Reno 13 (or 14)? Especially for daylight photos, low light, and video recording — is there a big difference?


I’d really appreciate feedback from anyone who’s switched from Samsung to Oppo recently — especially if you care about privacy features, useful tweaks, camera performance, and daily usability.

Thanks in advance!
Translated:
Hi everyone,

I’ve been using a Samsung Galaxy A71 for the last 3 years (running One UI 5), and honestly, it’s still a solid phone. But I’m thinking of upgrading soon and have narrowed it down to two options:

1. Galaxy A56


2. Oppo Reno 13 (or maybe wait for the Reno 14, which isn’t out yet in Pakistan)

Before I decide, I have a few questions for people who’ve used ColorOS 15:

1. Does ColorOS have anything like Samsung’s Secure Folder, where you can keep private apps and photos separate?

2. Does it offer Modes and Routines, or something similar to automate settings based on time, location, etc.?

3. Is there any feature to lower the minimum volume? I usually fall asleep listening to podcasts, and I find Samsung’s lowest volume still too loud sometimes.

4. How good is the ColorOS Gallery app compared to Samsung’s?

Can you hide photos/videos easily?

Can you create custom albums?

Can you rename photos/videos directly in the app?

5. Does the file manager let you sync or access files directly from Google Drive?

6. Can you clone apps (e.g., use two WhatsApp/Facebook accounts on the same phone) easily?

7. Lastly, how does the camera quality compare between the Galaxy A56 and Oppo Reno 13 (or 14)? Especially for daylight photos, low light, and video recording — is there a big difference?


I’d really appreciate feedback from anyone who’s switched from Samsung to Oppo recently — especially if you care about privacy features, useful tweaks, camera performance, and daily usability.

Thanks in advance!

My usage:
• 3 years minimum
• 6+ hrs daily
• ~100 photos/day
• No gaming — only smoothness, camera & long-term stability
• Heavy use of OriginOS Private Space (To separate my Personal life with my Professional life in an one phone.)

X200 FE costs ~₹55–60K, while X300 goes ~₹80–85K.
Do you think paying extra for X300 truly makes sense for real-world 3-year usage?

Text:
Smaller screens generally lead to higher "Accommodative Demand," which can accelerate fatigue and contribute to long-term focusing issues.
Here is a breakdown of why the 6.1" screen is more "straining" than a 6.8" screen in the long term:
1. The "Angular Subtense" Problem
When a screen is smaller, the letters occupy a smaller angle on your retina. To compensate for small text, our natural instinct is not to squint, but to bring the device closer to our face.
The 6.1" Habit: You might hold it at 25 cm to see clearly.
The 6.8" Habit: You might be comfortable holding it at 35 cm.
The Result: Moving from 35 cm to 25 cm doesn't just increase effort slightly; it increases the accommodative demand exponentially. This keeps you trapped in the "Red Zone"(<30cm).

मैं आपका वीडियो नियमित रूप से देखता हूँ और आपकी बातों से मुझे बहुत motivation मिलता है 📱📚। आपने हमेशा सही guidance और सच्ची जानकारी दी है, जिससे मैं अपनी पढ़ाई और future को लेकर और serious हुआ हूँ।

अगर आप मुझे एक फोन देकर सहयोग कर सकें, तो मैं उसे सिर्फ एक gadget नहीं बल्कि अपने सपनों को पूरा करने का एक जरिया मानूँगा। मैं वादा करता हूँ कि आपकी इस मदद को मैं अपनी मेहनत से सही साबित करूँगा और एक दिन आपको गर्व महसूस कराऊँगा।

Hello sir 🙏
I watch your videos regularly and I get a lot of motivation from your words 📱📚. You have always given correct guidance and true information, due to which I have become more serious about my studies and future.

If you can support me by giving me a phone, I will consider it not just a gadget but a means to fulfill my dreams. I promise that I will justify your help with my hard work and make you feel proud one day.
thank you sir 🙏

🚨 Whatever machines they show off, I will never consider any nothing in my entire life.
It was june 2025 i bought a CMF1 from Flipkart - i was very happy with the Os and it's features.
But in August 2025 - just after 56 days the phone got into Loop restart problem - I wasn't worried because it might be caused by an update - but it got worse and I couldn't turn on my phone. I sent it to Guwahati from Tezpur 300 kms away from my home because they didn't have any service centre near me. But now is the interesting part - i called the service centre and they taught me some shit arguments and kind of forced me to believe that i dropped the phone into water and made damages. And they can not fix it under warranty and i have to pay 9600 rs for the motherboard. I didn't repair because i know it was not my fault they played with me.
Now i am using iphone - altho i was not in a point to afford an iphone at that time - but nothing taught me a good life lesson. And my iphone 16 is working fine - I'm paying in EMi's
